name: Biotite (Computational Molecular Biology)

performance.notes: NumPy/Cython optimized; fast for sequence and structure analysis

agent_readiness.notes: Unified computational biology library for sequences and structures. import biotite.sequence as seq. import biotite.structure as struc. import biotite.structure.io as strucio. Sequence: dna = seq.NucleotideSequence('ATCG'). rna = dna.transcribe(). protein = rna.translate(). Alignment: from biotite.sequence.align import align_optimal, SubstitutionMatrix. matrix = SubstitutionMatrix.std_protein_matrix('BLOSUM62'). ali = align_optimal(seq1, seq2, matrix). Structure: atom_array = strucio.load_structure('protein.pdb'). backbone = atom_array[struc.filter_backbone(atom_array)]. struc.rmsd(ref, mobile). struc.superimpose(ref, mobile). Visualization: biotite.visualize (matplotlib-based). Databases: NCBI, RCSB via biotite.database.

gotchas: Different design philosophy than BioPython — AtomArray vs PDB record objects
DSSP secondary structure requires dssp binary installed
Visualization module requires matplotlib but not interactive
best_when: Sequence analysis: alignment, translation, codon usage, sequence statistics
Structure analysis: RMSD, superimposition, distance calculation, atom selection
When NumPy-style array operations on molecular data are preferred
Cleaner API than BioPython for common computational biology tasks
avoid_when: When BioPython's extensive parser support is needed
summary: Biotite provides unified computational biology for sequences and structures. NucleotideSequence('ATCG').transcribe().translate(). align_optimal(seq1, seq2, BLOSUM62). strucio.load_structure('protein.pdb'). struc.rmsd(), superimpose(). AtomArray selection. NCBI/RCSB database access. BSD-3 by Patrick Kunzmann. Cleaner NumPy-style API than BioPython. DSSP requires binary. Limited non-standard residue support.
